review_cadence: review before every release-readiness run, after every remediation gate transition, and before any external claim change

stages.label: Design-partner alpha
stages.intent: Controlled, named design-partner evaluation of the current single-node semantic kernel while the remediation programme is executed.
stages.claim_boundary: Controlled single-node alpha with a real Rust semantic kernel, limited to one visibility domain, trusted appenders, and explicitly supported deployment boundaries.

gates.title: The controlled-alpha semantic boundary is explicit
next_actions: Keep the claim limited to one visibility domain and trusted appenders until the R1-R3 semantic gates pass.

gates.title: The supported alpha deployment boundary is explicit
next_actions: Keep SQLite as the packaged default.
Keep optional Postgres and non-loopback network paths outside the supported external claim until the transport-security gate passes.

gates.title: Hardening promotion state is explicit
blockers: Hardening evidence does not promote the claim beyond controlled alpha while the remediation gates remain blocked.
next_actions: Keep hardening findings visible and feed correctness-relevant failures into the stable remediation gates.

stages.label: Commercial beta
stages.intent: Future selected commercial beta, requalified only after every non-waivable remediation gate passes for one exact candidate and package.
stages.claim_boundary: Blocked. Commercial beta must not be claimed until the R7 exact-candidate qualification bundle verifies.

gates.title: Policy-scoped semantic correctness
blockers: Policy is still applied after resolution or derivation on correctness-bearing paths, so hidden facts can alter visible truth.
next_actions: Complete R1 and pass semantic.policy_noninterference without retrying correctness failures into green.

gates.title: Execution-scoped trace identity
blockers: Service-level explanation identity is process-local and tuple IDs can alias across executions.
next_actions: Complete R2 and pass semantic.trace_handle_identity across runs, appends, restarts, namespaces, and policy scopes.

gates.title: Transactional schema-valid append
blockers: Append accepts data before namespace-schema validation and can poison later replay.
next_actions: Complete R3 and pass storage.transactional_schema_append on every backend and write ingress.

gates.title: Immutable release evidence
blockers: Readiness is derived from authored statuses and path existence rather than immutable outcomes for the exact candidate.
next_actions: Complete R4 and verify one exact-SHA evidence bundle independently.

gates.title: Dependency SBOM, vulnerability, and license evidence
blockers: The current package manifest is not a dependency SBOM, and vulnerability, license, and code-scanning gates are absent.
next_actions: Complete the R5 supply-chain controls and verify the final package and SBOM provenance.

gates.title: Supported transport-security boundary
blockers: Remote Postgres cannot verify TLS and the supported non-loopback HTTP transport boundary is not yet enforced.
next_actions: Complete the R5 transport contract and pass TLS identity, downgrade-rejection, and secret-redaction tests.

stages.label: General availability
stages.intent: Broad commercial release with repeatable artifact promotion, support posture, security posture, and conservative public claims.
stages.claim_boundary: AETHER can be sold beyond named design partners with supported deployment, upgrade, security, observability, and incident-response commitments. Distributed runtime claims still require separately gated evidence.

gates.title: Signed artifact and promotion pipeline
blockers: Release-readiness is still a pre-release verification flow, not a signed artifact promotion pipeline.
No GA-grade versioned changelog, artifact signing, or provenance attestation is enforced.
next_actions: Add signed artifact generation and verification.
Tie release promotion to a versioned changelog and immutable release evidence bundle.

gates.title: Support, incident, and disclosure posture
blockers: Responsible disclosure exists, but public bug bounty and customer support commitments are not yet in force.
Incident response and escalation playbooks are not yet formalized for external customers.
next_actions: Create customer incident-response and escalation playbooks.
Decide if and when to launch a paid public bug bounty.

gates.title: Multi-platform distribution is supported
blockers: Linux/macOS package parity and installation verification are not yet GA gates.
next_actions: Add Linux and macOS package builders or explicitly scope GA to one supported platform.
Publish install and rollback evidence per supported platform.

gates.title: Distributed truth claims are separately gated
blockers: Replicated authority partitions are a single-host manual-failover prototype.
No automatic election, quorum consensus, multi-host failover, or replicated sidecar plane is implemented.
next_actions: Keep distributed runtime language out of GA claims until separately proven.
Continue exact-provenance partition work without pulling Raft or broad consensus into the beta gate.
